meaning: adj.犹太教所规定允许的,合适的vt.使合适
examples: Acting was not a kosher trade for an upper-class girl.
At the kosher dinner table in her home near Cleveland, she said that she would start arguments with her parents so she could stomp off and avoid eating.
Religious Jews buy only kosher-certified products and they separate their meat and dairy foods.
Not long ago it opened a kosher kitchen that serves 300 meals a day.
